Home Tours Attractions Regions Food & Culture About Contact

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about travelling to Afghanistan with us

Category

Access

Is the Friday Mosque open to tourists?

Yes, we coordinate visits outside of prayer times to ensure respectful access to the tile-work and courtyards.

Category

Connectivity

Are there restrictions on social media?

Some platforms are intermittently blocked. We recommend a VPN. Avoid posting live locations; it is safer to post after leaving a district.

Category

Culture

Is Kandahar safe for Westerners?

With our 15-year network and local guides, we manage a safe, respectful, and high-standard visit to the Pashtun heartland.

Category

Etiquette

Can I travel as a solo woman?

Yes, but with caveats. Under 2026 regulations, women must be accompanied by a 'Mahram' for long-distance travel. Our lead guide often fulfills this role.

Is music or dancing allowed?

Public performances are restricted. We ask guests to use headphones. In private traditional settings, some local folk music may be experienced discreetly.

Can I bring a drone?

Drones are strictly regulated. Bringing one without a Ministry of Defense permit usually results in confiscation. Permit processes take 3 months.

Traveling during Ramadan?

Eating or drinking in public during daylight is illegal. We adjust schedules to provide private dining spaces for guests during the day.

Category

Fitness

What is the required skiing ability?

This is backcountry skiing at high altitude. You should be a confident intermediate to advanced off-piste skier.

Category

Food

Is vegetarian or vegan food available?

Vegetarianism is uncommon but 'Borani Banjan' and 'Ashak' are staples. Veganism is harder due to yogurt/ghee, but we can coordinate meals.

Category

Health

What happens in a medical emergency?

Our protocols rely on your mandatory 'High-Risk' insurance for stabilization and rapid medical evacuation to Dubai or Istanbul.

Category

Insurance

Is high-risk insurance mandatory?

Yes. Standard travel insurance does not cover Afghanistan. You must have specialized 'High-Risk' insurance including Medical Evacuation.

Category

Leadership

What makes Afghan Tours unique?

Our leadership includes a US citizen who has lived and worked in Kabul for 15 years, providing an unmatched local network and safety oversight.

Category

Logistics

Do I need to bring my own skis?

We recommend bringing your own boots and skins for fit, but we can facilitate local equipment rental if arranged in advance.

How do we sleep during the trek?

Accommodation is a mix of high-quality expedition tents and traditional Pamiri 'guest houses'.

Category

Money

Can I use my credit cards?

No. Afghanistan is a cash-only economy. You must bring clean, post-2013 USD bills to exchange for Afghanis (AFN).

What should I do with leftover Afghanis?

The Afghani (AFN) is difficult to exchange outside. We recommend spending it on souvenirs or exchanging it back to USD at Sarai Shahzada.

Category

Photography

Are there restrictions on what I can film?

We manage permits for professional gear. Military sites and certain government buildings are strictly off-limits.

Category

Planning

How flexible is the itinerary?

We are agile. While we have a planned route, we finalize details close to your arrival to account for the most current security and weather data.

Category

Preparation

What kind of training do I need?

This is a high-altitude trek. You should be capable of walking 6-8 hours a day on rugged terrain above 3,000m.

Category

Safety

What is the 'Low-Profile' travel policy?

We use unmarked, standard local vehicles (like Toyota Corollas) rather than luxury SUVs to blend in and reduce unwanted attention.

Category

Timing

When do Buzkashi matches take place?

Matches usually happen on Fridays during the cooler months. We track local schedules in real-time.

Category

Visa

How do I obtain an Afghan visa?

You need a Letter of Invitation (LOI) from us. You then apply at an Afghan consulate in hubs like Dubai, Islamabad, or Istanbul.

Category

Visas

What is the 'Maktub' and do I need it?

A 'Maktub' is an internal provincial permit. While your visa gets you into the country, the Maktub allows you to pass checkpoints between provinces. We handle all Maktub applications.

Still Have Questions?

We're happy to answer anything. Reach out directly and we'll respond within 24 hours.

Contact Us
AfghanTours

Discover the breathtaking landscapes, ancient history, and rich culture of Afghanistan with our expert-guided tours.
